column2: A Windows volume has logical file-system errors, but there is no evidence of bad sectors. Which Check Disk option is the better first repair choice?
column5: The /f option targets logical file-system repair. Use /r when the task also requires bad-sector checking and readable-data recovery.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic HighYield CLI Disk Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician suspects a Windows drive has bad sectors, not just logical file-system errors. Which Check Disk option fits that scenario?
column5: /r includes the /f behavior and adds bad-sector checking and readable-data recovery, so it is broader than a logical repair-only check.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic HighYield CLI Disk Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician is preparing a partition for reuse and is warned that the action can remove access to existing data. Which command category fits this task?
column5: Formatting prepares the target for use with Windows. It is not a general repair command, and the technician must confirm the target and data requirements first.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic HighYield CLI Disk Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs command-line access to disk, partition, and volume configuration. What risk should they keep in mind before using DiskPart?
column4: DiskPart can destroy data if the wrong disk, partition, or volume is selected.
column5: DiskPart is appropriate for disk configuration work, but the safety issue is the main distinction from informational commands.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic HighYield CLI Disk Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs a more capable command-line copy tool for a structured file copy between folders or systems. Why is Robust Copy the better fit than older copy utilities?
column4: It is designed as a robust Windows file-copy tool for more capable copy jobs.
column5: The typed Command card tests the exact command. This card tests when the robust copy tool is the right file-management choice.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic CLI Files Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician is troubleshooting a permissions difference between two Windows sessions. Why is the detailed whoami output useful?
column4: It shows the current user context plus detailed group and privilege information.
column5: Plain whoami identifies the current user. The detailed form is better when the problem may depend on group membership or privileges.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic CLI Identity Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs a Windows client to process Group Policy updates now instead of waiting for normal refresh timing. Which Group Policy command role fits this task: update or results reporting?
column5: gpupdate applies policy updates. Use gpresult when the task is to report which policies applied.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic HighYield CLI GroupPolicy Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs to verify which Group Policy settings apply to a computer or user. Which Group Policy command role fits this task: update or results reporting?
column5: gpresult reports policy results. It does not force a new policy refresh; that is the job of gpupdate.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic HighYield CLI GroupPolicy Source::Messer-v140

column2: After malware cleanup, a technician wants to check whether protected Windows system files still have integrity. What command-line tool category fits this?
column5: System File Checker is for protected system-file integrity. It is not for disk partitioning, network-share mapping, or DNS lookup.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic HighYield CLI Repair Source::Messer-v140

column2: A Windows client has an IP address, but the technician also needs DNS and DHCP server details. Why use the expanded ipconfig output instead of the basic output?
column4: It includes additional adapter configuration details such as DNS and DHCP server information.
column5: The typed Command card tests the exact command form. This card tests why the expanded output is selected during network troubleshooting.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 10
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic CLI Network Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician wants active connection information from a Windows client, but does not need DNS name lookups or route tracing. Which network command category fits best?
column5: netstat focuses on local network statistics and active connections. Use tracert or pathping for route-path questions, and nslookup for DNS.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 10
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic CLI Network Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs to assign a drive letter to a Windows share, while another technician needs account information. Which net command role fits the drive mapping task?
column5: This card tests the net use versus net user distinction. The typed Command card tests exact command recall.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 10
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic HighYield CLI Network Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs user account information, while another technician needs to map a network share. Which net command role fits the account-information task?
column5: This card tests the net user versus net use distinction. The typed Command card tests exact command recall.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 10
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic CLI Accounts Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician can reach a host, but wants to see the network path by hop. Which type of command is more appropriate than a basic reachability test?
column5: This distinction matters because ping tests basic reachability, while tracert shows the path by hop.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 10
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic HighYield CLI Network Source::Messer-v140

column2: A route trace shows the path, but the technician also needs round-trip and packet-loss statistics at each hop. Which route-analysis approach fits?
column5: pathping is useful when tracert alone is not enough because the technician also needs loss and timing data along the route.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.5; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 10
column10: A+::220-1202::1.5 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftCommandlineTools Basic HighYield CLI Network Source::Messer-v140
